reasoning: D wins on every axis: its five mechanics sit on genuinely separate conceptual axes (a wandering portable "down" in WICK, a vestibular duel in KEEL, momentum-banking in CLOT, propellant-as-consumable-ground in LICHEN, and a hardening lattice of frozen straight-line shots in SUTURE), and SUTURE in particular is the single most original and generative idea in the set, discovering that deleting the arc yields a geometry sport rather than a ballistics one. D also reasons hardest about actual playability, using the KEEL spectator problem to propose a head-up-display solution. A is a close second with the best prose craft and a standout bluffing-about-invisible-mass concept (SUMP) plus the socially-constructed-surface axis (SHROUD), but its axes overlap D's without exceeding them. C edges out B on the strength of PARALLAX, a truly novel perception-based sport built on the loss of a shared reference frame, though QUORUM and CAROM retread collective-balance and billiards ideas. B is the most competent but also the safest: ECHO is conventional ricochet-dodgeball and SECOND SKIN is a familiar rotating-shell trope, so it lands last despite clean execution.
